mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP (GDPC1) signals expansion of the U.S. economy, which drives higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly growth has zero or negative rank correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns over a rolling 2-year window (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity, leading to improved investor sentiment and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO monthly change is uncorrelated or negatively correlated with 60-day forward SPX returns (absolute Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital rises, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS level at decision time is 4.79%
regime_hint: monetary tightening when rates are above neutral
failure_condition: In instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time (breakeven ratio > 1).

mechanism_narrative: A level of CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 200 indicates high inflation that prompts the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, which is negative for equity prices over a 1-month horizon.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL > 200, 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the hit rate is not significantly different from 50% (binomial test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Combined signals of rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change > 0 and UNRATE MoM change < 0
failure_condition: When PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously, the 5-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 50% of occurrences over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer and investment demand, supporting economic growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM change > 2% and RSAFS MoM change > 1%
failure_condition: In months where both HOUST and RSAFS exceed their growth thresholds, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative or flat (Rank IC < 0.1) over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime indicated by elevated federal funds rate, strong industrial production signals potential overheating and further tightening, which hurts equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, INDPRO increases show a positive correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC > 0.05), contradicting the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-2008 recovery regime, strong GDP growth (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter > 2%) confirms sustainable expansion and boosts investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 1-week horizon.
failure_condition: In quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2%, the subsequent 5-day SPX return is negative in more than 60% of cases, or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10).
